zh.lifeSummary: 當Dirk Persijn van Putten於1118年出生在荷蘭努-拉藍德蒙尼肯達姆時，他的父親Dirk Jansz Persijn van Velsen49歲，母親Bertha Bourgogne56歲。他和Bertrade Arnoudsdr. Spiker van Waterland Erfvrouwe van Waterland至少有4個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於1179年在荷蘭努-拉藍德艾姆-賓享年61歲。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Putten
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Outen
meanings: in Hampshire and Sussex a topographic name from Middle English ove(n), uve(n) (Old English ofan, ufan) ‘above’ + Middle English toun (Old English tūn) ‘village’, denoting someone who lived at the upper or higher end of a village. It appears to be a type of formation particular to Sussex; compare Easton 2.

in Essex and East Anglia probably a variant of   Olton   alias   Oulton  , from either of two places called Oulton in Norfolk and Suffolk. The /l/ has been vocalized and absorbed into the preceding vowel.

nameMeanings.name: Dirk
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Flemish and Dutch form of Derek . Its use in the English-speaking world since the 1960s is largely due to the fame of the actor Dirk Bogarde ( 1921–99 , born Derek Niven van den Bogaerde ). He was of Dutch descent, although he was actually born in Scotland. The manly image of the name has been reinforced by its coincidence in form with Scottish dirk ‘dagger’ (from Gaelic durc).

nameMeanings.name: Persijn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Croatian (Peršin): from peršin ‘parsley’, probably used as a nickname of a grower or seller.
Slovenian (Peršin): augmentative derivative of the old personal name Perše, itself a derivative of Peter .
French: nickname derived from Old French pers ‘blue, livid, deathly pale’, probably denoting a man with pale complexion.

variants.name: Peter
meanings: form of the name of the best-known of all Christ's apostles, traditionally regarded as the founder of the Christian Church. The name derives, via Latin, from Greek petros ‘stone, rock’. This is used as a translation of the Aramaic byname Cephas, given to the apostle Simon son of Jona, to distinguish him from another of the same name ( Simon Zelotes ). ‘When Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone’ (John 1:42). According to Matthew 16:17–18, Christ says more explicitly, ‘Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona … thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church’. The name has been in continuous use since the Middle Ages.

en.lifeSummary: When Dirk Persijn van Putten was born in 1118, in Monnickendam, North Holland, Netherlands, his father, Dirk Jansz Persijn van Velsen, was 49 and his mother, Bertha Bourgogne, was 56. He had at least 4 sons and 1 daughter with Bertrade Arnoudsdr. Spiker van Waterland Erfvrouwe van Waterland. He died in 1179, in Egmond-Binnen, North Holland, Netherlands, at the age of 61.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Dirk Persijn van Putten est né en 1118, à Monnickendam, Hollande-septentrionale, Pays-Bas, son père, Dirk Jansz Persijn van Velsen, avait 49 ans et sa mère, Bertha Bourgogne, avait 56 ans. Il a eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille avec Bertrade Arnoudsdr. Spiker van Waterland Erfvrouwe van Waterland. Il est décédé en 1179, à Egmond-Binnen, Hollande-septentrionale, Pays-Bas, à l’âge de 61 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Dirk Persijn van Putten 1118 in Monnickendam, Nord-Holland, Niederlande geboren wurde, war sein Vater Dirk Jansz Persijn van Velsen 49 und seine Mutter Bertha Bourgogne 56. Er hatte mit Bertrade Arnoudsdr. Spiker van Waterland Erfvrouwe van Waterland mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1179 im Alter von 61 Jahren in Egmond-Binnen, Nord-Holland, Niederlande.

Lui avuto almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia con Bertrade Arnoudsdr. Spiker van Waterland Erfvrouwe van Waterland. Morì nel 1179, nel Egmond-Binnen, Noord-Holland, Paesi Bassi, all'età di 61 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Dirk Persijn van Putten(이)가 1118년 네덜란드 노르드-홀랜드 몬이c켄다에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Dirk Jansz Persijn van Velsen(은)는 49세였고, 그의 어머니 Bertha Bourgogne(은)는 56세였다. 그는 Bertrade Arnoudsdr. Spiker van Waterland Erfvrouwe van Waterland(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1179년 네덜란드 노르드-홀랜드 그몽-빈에서 61세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Dirk Persijn van Putten родился в 1118 г. в местности монниккендам, нурд-холланд, Нидерланды, его отцу по имени Dirk Jansz Persijn van Velsen было 49 лет, а матери, которую звали Bertha Bourgogne, 56. У него было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь от Bertrade Arnoudsdr. Spiker van Waterland Erfvrouwe van Waterland. Он умер в 1179 г. в местности эгмонд-биннен, нурд-холланд, Нидерланды в возрасте 61 год.

Tuvo por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija con Bertrade Arnoudsdr. Spiker van Waterland Erfvrouwe van Waterland. Murió en 1179, en Egmond-Binnen, Noord-Holland, Países Bajos, a la edad de 61 años.
